violations.text: The door to the outside, located in the front lobby, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/02/2025
Cases of food items were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in freezer. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/09/2025
The following was observed in poor repair: - 1 door cooler shelves (white flakey paint), - light bulb under hood vent, - walk-in cooler door gasket. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/09/2025

The following non-food contact surfaces have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and/or other debris: - prep units, - hood vents, - table under grill. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/25/2025

The outside dumpsters are not covered. Inspector observed one receptacle with an open lid and the second receptacle was in poor repair (no lids). Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/25/2025

The floors (including the walk-in cooler) and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/09/2025
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. New Violation. Correct By: 06/25/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Application provided. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2022

The following was observed: - A container was observed in the hand-washing sink near the pizza prep station. - Food particles (lettuce and olives) were observed in the hand-washing sink near the sandwich prep station. Hand-washing sinks must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. PIC informed and removed items. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/25/2025
The hand-washing sink in the men's restroom was lacking proper hand-washing signage. New Violation. Correct By: 06/25/2025

The following food contact surfaces were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. - can opener, - small food chopper, - large food chopper, - measuring cup. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC placed items in 3 bay sink to be washed and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/25/2025

Frozen commercially cooked meatballs and tomato sauce were only were observed reheating in steam-table at 74 degrees F. Ready to eat foods taken from a commercially processed can or intact package from a food processing plant must be heated to at least 135 degrees Fahrenheit for hot holding. PIC informed to end that practice. PIC placed item on flat top grill to reheat. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/25/2025

Diced tomatoes were observed held between 49-50 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC discarded. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/25/2025

Deli meat and cheese were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 06/25/2025
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/15/2022

Window cleaner spray bottle was stored with food, equipment, utensils, or single service items. Chemicals must be stored to prevent the contamination of clean equipment, utensils linens, and/or single service by spacing or partitioning and locating them in an area that is not above food, equipment utensils, linens, and single service articles. PIC relocated bottle. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/25/2025

Working containers of dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 06/25/2025

The door to the outside, located in the front lobby, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 07/02/2025
Various food items were observed stored on the floor in the kitchen. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 06/25/2025
Wet wiping cloths are not stored in a chemical sanitizer between uses. Wet wiping cloths must be stored in a chemical sanitizer at the appropriate concentration. New Violation. Correct By: 06/25/2025

Cup was being used as scoops in containers of flour. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. New Violation. Correct By: 06/25/2025

Plastic eating utensils in the self-serve area are displayed with the lip surface up. Utensils are to be stored so that only the handles are touched by employees and consumers if consumer self-service is provided. New Violation. Correct By: 06/25/2025
The following was not in good repair:- 1 door cooler shelves (white flakey paint),- light bulb under hood vent,- walk-in cooler door gasket. New Violation. Correct By: 06/25/2025

Cardboard is used in the construction of shelf liners in the walk-in cooler and on top of pizza oven. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 06/25/2025

The following non-food contact surfaces have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and/or other debris:- prep units,- hood vents,- freezer lowboy,- table under grill,- walk-in shelves,- walk-in cooler fan blades- 1 door cooler. New Violation. Correct By: 06/25/2025
Inspector observed a heavy accumulation of trash and debris around refuse area. Area to be maintained clean. New Violation. Correct By: 06/25/2025
The outside dumpster is not covered. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. New Violation. Correct By: 06/25/2025

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 06/25/2025
